name: Las Navas de la Concepción from Constantina – circular through the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park
name_original: Las Navas de la Concepción desde Constantina – circular por el Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla
region_intro: Touring cycling around Constantina offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. The region features varied terrain, transitioning from the Guadalquivir valley to the hills of Sierra Morena, with elevations ranging from 10 meters to 700 meters. Routes wind through Mediterranean forests, extensive olive groves, and pastures, often following rivers and streams. This area provides a mix of gentle descents and challenging ascents for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Constantina The most popular touring cycling route is Via Verde de la Sierra Norte de Sevilla – circular from Constantina, a 22.2 miles (35.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 28 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows an old railway line, offering a mostly paved and accessible cycling experience. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Las Navas de la Concepción from Constantina – circular through the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, a difficult 35.2 miles (56.7 km) path. This route leads through the natural park, showcasing varied elevations and scenic views of the region's diverse landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Cerro del Hierro – Isla Margarita Recreation Area loop from Cerro del Hierro, a 21.6 miles (34.8 km) trail leading through the Cerro del Hierro Natural Monument, often completed in about 2 hours 10 minutes. Touring cycling around Constantina is defined by Mediterranean forests, olive groves, and the unique karstic formations of Cerro del Hierro. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Constantina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Constantina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Constantina?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes available around Constantina, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a diverse selection, from easy rides to more challenging adventures through the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Constantina?
A: Constantina offers a varied cycling terrain, transitioning from the Guadalquivir valley to the hills of Sierra Morena. You can expect elevations ranging from 10 meters to 700 meters, with routes winding through picturesque Mediterranean forests, extensive olive groves, and pastures. Many paths also follow rivers and streams, providing scenic views.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the touring routes in Constantina?
A: The touring cycling routes in Constantina are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful natural landscapes, and the well-maintained paths, especially the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988036636/via-verde-de-la-sierra-norte-de-sevilla-circular-from-constantina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de la Sierra Norte de Sevilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Constantina?
A: Yes, Constantina offers several routes suitable for beginners. While most routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy touring cycling route. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988036636/via-verde-de-la-sierra-norte-de-sevilla-circular-from-constantina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de la Sierra Norte de Sevilla – circular from Constantina&lt;/a&gt;, built on an old railway line, provides a mostly paved and accessible cycling experience with gentle gradients, making it a great option for those new to touring cycling.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Constantina offers numerous difficult routes. There are 39 difficult touring cycling routes in the area. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990396629/las-navas-de-la-concepcion-from-constantina-circular-through-the-sierra-norte-de-sevilla-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Navas de la Concepción from Constantina – circular through the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 35.2 miles (56.7 km) and features significant elevation changes through the natural park's diverse landscapes.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Constantina?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Constantina are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988036636/via-verde-de-la-sierra-norte-de-sevilla-circular-from-constantina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de la Sierra Norte de Sevilla – circular from Constantina&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 22.2-mile (35.8 km) loop.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes in Constantina?
A: The touring cycling routes in Constantina pass through the stunning Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, offering views of Mediterranean forests, olive groves, and various water features. You can encounter the unique karstic formations of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4781135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Hierro Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, cycle along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4781503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Norte de Sevilla Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, and enjoy the serene landscapes around the Huéznar River and its reservoirs like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/564942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;José Torán Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2676623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéznar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest to explore by bike?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4781135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Hierro Natural Monument&lt;/a&gt;, a former mining site with unique rock formations, is a significant landmark. The town of Constantina itself is a Bien de Interés Cultural, featuring a historic center and a castle. The routes also offer views of various reservoirs and the picturesque Huéznar River landscape.

Q: Can I cycle near waterfalls in the Constantina area?
A: Yes, you can cycle in areas that offer access to waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4764750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéznar Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; are a notable natural feature in the region, located near the source of the Huéznar River. Some routes may pass close enough for a short detour to experience this refreshing natural spectacle.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Constantina?
A: Yes, the region offers family-friendly options, particularly the Vía Verde de la Sierra Norte de Sevilla. This greenway, built on an old railway line, provides a mostly paved and moderate cycling experience, making it suitable for families with children. It offers a safe and enjoyable way to explore the natural park.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Constantina?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Constantina is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. The landscapes are also particularly vibrant in spring.

Q: Are there options for parking near the touring cycling routes in Constantina?
A: Yes, parking options are generally available near the starting points of many touring cycling routes in Constantina and within the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. For example, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6887455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Hierro – Isla Margarita Recreation Area loop from Cerro del Hierro&lt;/a&gt; often have designated parking areas at trailheads or nearby recreation zones.
tips: Also called "Cerro Negrillo" viewpoint - it is a balcony-viewpoint located in the central-eastern area of the Natural Park, where you can contemplate the beauty of the Sierra Norte de Sevilla. Nice road until you reach the viewpoint. The name "Puerto del Robledo" is due to the presence of oak trees. The "Cerro Negrito" is the highest level of this sector (903 masl). As a curiosity - it is the rainiest place in the province of Seville.

The origin of this hermitage dates back to the Sevillian Mudejar from which the three main naves separated by pointed arches come from. The various parts of the building were built at different times, with some Baroque and Neoclassical elements.
The Moorish-era castle was declared a site of cultural interest in 1985. It can be easily reached on foot and by car. The panoramic views of the town are impressive.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of the Virgin of Robledo
